title: Gonolobus maritimus
intro: Gonolobus maritimus, also known as Sea Vine, is a flowering plant species within the Apocynaceae family, native to the coastal regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gonolobus maritimus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Gonolobus maritimus is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to the Americas and belongs to the genus Gonolobus.
